sbom_components.purpose: Starts the device and loads firmware
sbom_components.exploitability_context: Security-critical first-stage code. A bypass could allow unsigned firmware or rollback.
sbom_components.network_exposure: No direct network service; exposed through firmware update package, debug/service interface, or physical access.
sbom_components.compensating_controls: Secure boot, signed images, anti-rollback counter, debug lock, production fuse settings.

sbom_components.exploitability_context: Controls measurements, alarms, UI, storage, and communications. Compromise could affect safety and data integrity.
sbom_components.network_exposure: Indirectly exposed through BLE/Wi-Fi/USB/cloud interfaces and update process.
sbom_components.compensating_controls: Signed firmware, memory protection, watchdog, secure coding review, static analysis, release validation.

sbom_components.exploitability_context: RTOS defects can affect task isolation, timing, availability, and memory safety.
sbom_components.network_exposure: No direct exposure unless network stacks or external interfaces run as RTOS tasks.
sbom_components.compensating_controls: Minimal RTOS configuration, stack overflow checks, watchdog, task priority review, patch monitoring.

sbom_components.purpose: Interfaces with sensors, display, buttons, storage
sbom_components.exploitability_context: Driver flaws can corrupt readings, crash the device, or mishandle peripheral input.
sbom_components.network_exposure: Local peripheral buses only; indirect exposure through malformed external inputs.
sbom_components.compensating_controls: Input bounds checks, bus fault handling, driver unit tests, hardware abstraction review.

sbom_components.exploitability_context: Tampering or defects can produce inaccurate SpO2 or pulse results.
sbom_components.network_exposure: No network exposure; uses local sensor data from acquisition path.
sbom_components.compensating_controls: Algorithm checksum, clinical regression suite, calibration controls, plausibility checks.

sbom_components.exploitability_context: Manipulated sampling timing or LED drive control can affect measurement accuracy.
sbom_components.network_exposure: Local hardware control path; indirect exposure through configuration or firmware changes.
sbom_components.compensating_controls: Protected calibration settings, sampling sanity checks, acquisition watchdog, configuration signing.

sbom_components.exploitability_context: Suppressed or delayed alarms can create direct patient safety risk.
sbom_components.network_exposure: No public network service; receives local measurement and settings inputs.
sbom_components.compensating_controls: Independent alarm checks, locked alarm limits, audit logging, local alarm priority over cloud functions.

sbom_components.exploitability_context: UI flaws can permit unauthorized setting changes or misleading displayed values.
sbom_components.network_exposure: Local physical interface; indirect exposure through companion app or service workflow if linked.
sbom_components.compensating_controls: Role-based screens, confirmation prompts, input validation, screen lock, audit logs.

sbom_components.exploitability_context: Unauthorized changes could alter alarm behavior or clinical workflow.
sbom_components.network_exposure: Local UI, BLE setup, service tool, or cloud configuration path if enabled.
sbom_components.compensating_controls: Signed configuration, access control, change audit trail, default restore, integrity checks.

sbom_components.exploitability_context: Storage compromise can expose or alter patient measurements.
sbom_components.network_exposure: No direct network listener; data may be exported through USB, BLE, Wi-Fi, or cloud sync.
sbom_components.compensating_controls: Encrypted storage, integrity tags, least-privilege access, secure erase, export authorization.

sbom_components.exploitability_context: Incorrect access control or indexing may expose patient-associated sessions.
sbom_components.network_exposure: Indirect exposure through UI, export, hospital integration, mobile app, or cloud upload.
sbom_components.compensating_controls: Patient data minimization, access controls, encrypted identifiers, audit logging.

sbom_components.exploitability_context: Aggregates external interfaces and is a major attack surface.
sbom_components.network_exposure: Exposed through enabled USB, BLE, Wi-Fi, Ethernet, and cloud channels.
sbom_components.compensating_controls: Interface hardening, protocol allow-list, authentication, firewall rules, fuzz testing.

sbom_components.exploitability_context: BLE pairing and GATT authorization weaknesses can allow unauthorized local access.
sbom_components.network_exposure: BLE radio exposure during pairing or authorized communication.
sbom_components.compensating_controls: Short pairing window, numeric comparison, bonded-device whitelist, encrypted pairing, rate limits.

sbom_components.exploitability_context: Wi-Fi client vulnerabilities or misconfiguration can expose credentials or disrupt telemetry.
sbom_components.network_exposure: Wi-Fi radio, WPA2/WPA3 negotiation, outbound cloud network.
sbom_components.compensating_controls: WPA2/WPA3, credential encryption, no ad-hoc mode, network allow-list, update monitoring.

sbom_components.exploitability_context: Malformed packets can cause denial of service or memory corruption if the stack is exposed.
sbom_components.network_exposure: Network-facing when Wi-Fi/Ethernet is enabled.
sbom_components.compensating_controls: Disable unused services, packet filtering, fuzz tests, segmentation, watchdog recovery.

sbom_components.exploitability_context: USB can be abused for unauthorized service access, data export, or malicious update attempts.
sbom_components.network_exposure: Physical USB/service port exposure.
sbom_components.compensating_controls: Service unlock token, signed update import, disabled mass storage by default, audit log.

sbom_components.exploitability_context: File system defects can corrupt logs, settings, measurements, or update staging data.
sbom_components.network_exposure: No direct network exposure; receives local writes from firmware and update manager.
sbom_components.compensating_controls: Wear leveling, integrity checks, backup settings copy, safe commit pattern, fault injection tests.

sbom_components.exploitability_context: Log tampering or loss can hide attacks, failures, or unauthorized changes.
sbom_components.network_exposure: Local storage and export interfaces; cloud upload if enabled.
sbom_components.compensating_controls: Append-only audit events, timestamp integrity, log export authorization, hash chaining.

sbom_components.exploitability_context: If disabled or bypassed, faults and security-relevant anomalies may go undetected.
sbom_components.network_exposure: No network exposure; local diagnostic path.
sbom_components.compensating_controls: Fail-safe states, fault counters, watchdog integration, diagnostic event logging.

sbom_components.exploitability_context: Timestamp manipulation can weaken audit trails and clinical event reconstruction.
sbom_components.network_exposure: RTC, cloud time sync, service tool time set, or mobile app time sync.
sbom_components.compensating_controls: Authenticated time source, monotonic counters, time-change audit events, drift detection.

sbom_components.exploitability_context: Bypassed self-tests may allow unsafe or compromised device operation.
sbom_components.network_exposure: No direct network exposure; runs at startup and maintenance workflows.
sbom_components.compensating_controls: Power-on self-test, sensor/display/buzzer checks, firmware integrity checks, failure lockout.

sbom_components.exploitability_context: Update path is high value for persistence or malicious firmware installation.
sbom_components.network_exposure: Cloud OTA, USB package import, or service tool update path.
sbom_components.compensating_controls: Signed packages, TLS, rollback prevention, staged update, recovery partition, update audit logs.

sbom_components.exploitability_context: Weak authentication can permit unauthorized configuration, export, or service operations.
sbom_components.network_exposure: Local UI, service tool, companion app, or cloud admin workflow.
sbom_components.compensating_controls: Password/PIN policy, lockout, token expiry, secure pairing, no default credentials.

sbom_components.exploitability_context: Authorization flaws can allow low-privilege users to perform restricted clinical or service actions.
sbom_components.network_exposure: Local UI, service tool, app, and remote management operations.
sbom_components.compensating_controls: Role-based access control, least privilege, server-side policy checks, audit logs.

sbom_components.exploitability_context: Crypto flaws can undermine TLS, signatures, and protected data storage.
sbom_components.network_exposure: Used by outbound TLS, update signature verification, and local encryption features.
sbom_components.compensating_controls: Disable weak ciphers, certificate pinning, patch monitoring, crypto configuration review.

sbom_components.supplier: Microchip Technology Inc. / Device OEM Integration
sbom_components.exploitability_context: Key theft can enable impersonation, forged updates, or decrypted data.
sbom_components.network_exposure: No direct network exposure; accessed locally by firmware crypto services.
sbom_components.compensating_controls: Hardware secure element, non-exportable keys, access counters, certificate rotation.

sbom_components.exploitability_context: Weak session validation can allow interception or spoofing.
sbom_components.network_exposure: Outbound TLS, BLE encrypted sessions, cloud/mobile communications.
sbom_components.compensating_controls: Mutual TLS, certificate pinning, BLE secure connections, replay protection, session timeout.

sbom_components.supplier: Device OEM / Cloud Device Management Team
sbom_components.exploitability_context: Remote management increases impact if cloud identity or command validation fails.
sbom_components.network_exposure: Outbound cloud channel; no inbound remote shell assumed.
sbom_components.compensating_controls: Signed commands, device allow-list, least privilege, command audit trail, staged rollout.

sbom_components.exploitability_context: Poor API validation can enable unauthorized access, data leakage, or command abuse.
sbom_components.network_exposure: Local app API, service API, cloud API gateway, hospital integration endpoints.
sbom_components.compensating_controls: Authentication, schema validation, rate limiting, authorization checks, API logging.

sbom_components.exploitability_context: Misrouting or weak transport security can leak telemetry or patient-associated data.
sbom_components.network_exposure: Outbound HTTPS/MQTT over TLS to cloud endpoints.
sbom_components.compensating_controls: mTLS, endpoint allow-list, payload minimization, retry limits, SIEM monitoring.

sbom_components.exploitability_context: Companion app pairing or API flaws may allow unauthorized configuration or data access.
sbom_components.network_exposure: BLE and app-to-cloud interface.
sbom_components.compensating_controls: Secure pairing, token binding, session timeout, app attestation where supported, audit logs.

sbom_components.exploitability_context: Hospital network integration can broaden attack scope and data exposure.
sbom_components.network_exposure: HL7/FHIR gateway or hospital network connector when enabled.
sbom_components.compensating_controls: Network segmentation, authenticated gateway, least data sharing, integration logging, allow-listed endpoints.

sbom_components.exploitability_context: Tampered audit logs can hide unauthorized actions or failed controls.
sbom_components.network_exposure: Local storage, service export, cloud upload if enabled.
sbom_components.compensating_controls: Append-only records, hash chaining, access control, secure time, export audit trail.

sbom_components.exploitability_context: License bypass may activate unvalidated or unauthorized features.
sbom_components.network_exposure: Local license file, service tool, or cloud entitlement sync.
sbom_components.compensating_controls: Signed licenses, feature gating, entitlement audit, offline grace policy, tamper checks.

sbom_components.exploitability_context: Disabled or misconfigured watchdog can reduce recovery from hangs or denial-of-service conditions.
sbom_components.network_exposure: No network exposure; monitors local tasks and hardware watchdog.
sbom_components.compensating_controls: Independent hardware watchdog, task health checks, reset reason logging, recovery tests.

cybersecurity_findings.patched_status: Requires review; NVD returned this CPE as affected and no local patch disposition is recorded in the SBOM.
cybersecurity_findings.exploitability_note: HIGH CVSS 8.8; attack vector LOCAL. Vulnerability: NVD classifies the CVE under CWE-284, NVD-CWE-Other; review the public description for deployment-specific impact. Weakness: CWE-284, NVD-CWE-Other. Exposure: Local or physically mediated exploitation should be assessed against service, update, or peripheral workflows. SBOM exposure: No direct exposure unless network stacks or external interfaces run as RTOS tasks. Controls: review vendor fix status, confirm exploit preconditions, and document whether compensating controls cover the affected interface. SBOM controls: Minimal RTOS configuration, stack overflow checks, watchdog, task priority review, patch monitoring.
cybersecurity_findings.description_summary: NVD describes memory-safety concern; review the CVE record for full advisory detail.
cybersecurity_findings.description_excerpt: FreeRTOS is a real-time operating system for microcontrollers. FreeRTOS Kernel versions through 10.6.1 do not sufficiently protect against local privilege es...

cybersecurity_findings.patched_status: Requires review; NVD returned this CPE as affected and no local patch disposition is recorded in the SBOM.
cybersecurity_findings.exploitability_note: MEDIUM CVSS 6.5; attack vector NETWORK. Vulnerability: NVD describes an authentication or access-control bypass condition in the affected component. Weakness: CWE-287. Exposure: Relevant when this component validates TLS peers, update endpoints, cloud links, or other certificate-backed channels. SBOM exposure: Wi-Fi radio, WPA2/WPA3 negotiation, outbound cloud network. Controls: update the TLS library, enforce strict certificate validation, and regression-test invalid certificate cases. SBOM controls: WPA2/WPA3, credential encryption, no ad-hoc mode, network allow-list, update monitoring.
cybersecurity_findings.description_summary: NVD describes secure-communication concern; review the CVE record for full advisory detail.
cybersecurity_findings.description_excerpt: The implementation of PEAP in wpa_supplicant through 2.10 allows authentication bypass. For a successful attack, wpa_supplicant must be configured to not ver...

cybersecurity_findings.patched_status: Requires review; NVD returned this CPE as affected and no local patch disposition is recorded in the SBOM.
cybersecurity_findings.exploitability_note: MEDIUM CVSS 6.8; attack vector NETWORK. Vulnerability: NVD describes incomplete TLS certificate-chain validation, where a connection may be accepted because one certificate in an otherwise untrusted chain is trusted. Weakness: CWE-287. Exposure: Relevant when this component validates TLS peers, update endpoints, cloud links, or other certificate-backed channels. SBOM exposure: Used by outbound TLS, update signature verification, and local encryption features. Controls: verify full certificate-chain validation, require trusted root and hostname match, and regression-test rejected-chain cases. SBOM controls: Disable weak ciphers, certificate pinning, patch monitoring, crypto configuration review.
cybersecurity_findings.description_summary: NVD describes remote exploitation context, secure-communication concern; review the CVE record for full advisory detail.
cybersecurity_findings.description_excerpt: Mutt 1.5.19, when linked against (1) OpenSSL (mutt_ssl.c) or (2) GnuTLS (mutt_ssl_gnutls.c), allows connections when only one TLS certificate in the chain is...

cybersecurity_findings.patched_status: Requires review; NVD returned this CPE as affected and no local patch disposition is recorded in the SBOM.
cybersecurity_findings.exploitability_note: MEDIUM CVSS 6.8; attack vector NETWORK. Vulnerability: NVD describes certificate host-name validation that mishandles embedded NUL characters in the certificate Common Name, enabling a crafted certificate-name mismatch. Weakness: CWE-310. Exposure: Relevant when this component validates TLS peers, update endpoints, cloud links, or other certificate-backed channels. SBOM exposure: Used by outbound TLS, update signature verification, and local encryption features. Controls: reject embedded NUL characters in certificate names, prefer patched TLS hostname-validation routines, and test crafted certificate names. SBOM controls: Disable weak ciphers, certificate pinning, patch monitoring, crypto configuration review.
cybersecurity_findings.description_summary: NVD describes secure-communication concern; review the CVE record for full advisory detail.
cybersecurity_findings.description_excerpt: mutt_ssl.c in mutt 1.5.19 and 1.5.20, when OpenSSL is used, does not properly handle a '\0' character in a domain name in the subject's Common Name (CN) fiel...

cybersecurity_findings.patched_status: Requires review; NVD returned this CPE as affected and no local patch disposition is recorded in the SBOM.
cybersecurity_findings.exploitability_note: MEDIUM CVSS 6.8; attack vector NETWORK. Vulnerability: NVD describes a certificate or TLS validation weakness in the affected secure-communication path. Weakness: CWE-310. Exposure: Relevant when this component validates TLS peers, update endpoints, cloud links, or other certificate-backed channels. SBOM exposure: Used by outbound TLS, update signature verification, and local encryption features. Controls: update the TLS library, enforce strict certificate validation, and regression-test invalid certificate cases. SBOM controls: Disable weak ciphers, certificate pinning, patch monitoring, crypto configuration review.
cybersecurity_findings.description_summary: NVD describes secure-communication concern; review the CVE record for full advisory detail.
cybersecurity_findings.description_excerpt: mutt_ssl.c in mutt 1.5.16 and other versions before 1.5.19, when OpenSSL is used, does not verify the domain name in the subject's Common Name (CN) field of...

cybersecurity_findings.patched_status: Requires review; NVD returned this CPE as affected and no local patch disposition is recorded in the SBOM.
cybersecurity_findings.exploitability_note: MEDIUM CVSS 4.3; attack vector NETWORK. Vulnerability: NVD describes certificate host-name validation that mishandles embedded NUL characters in the certificate Common Name, enabling a crafted certificate-name mismatch. Weakness: CWE-295. Exposure: Relevant when this component validates TLS peers, update endpoints, cloud links, or other certificate-backed channels. SBOM exposure: Used by outbound TLS, update signature verification, and local encryption features. Controls: reject embedded NUL characters in certificate names, prefer patched TLS hostname-validation routines, and test crafted certificate names. SBOM controls: Disable weak ciphers, certificate pinning, patch monitoring, crypto configuration review.
cybersecurity_findings.description_summary: NVD describes secure-communication concern; review the CVE record for full advisory detail.
cybersecurity_findings.description_excerpt: libraries/libldap/tls_o.c in OpenLDAP 2.2 and 2.4, and possibly other versions, when OpenSSL is used, does not properly handle a '\0' character in a domain n...

cybersecurity_findings.patched_status: Requires review; NVD returned this CPE as affected and no local patch disposition is recorded in the SBOM.
cybersecurity_findings.exploitability_note: HIGH CVSS 7.5; attack vector NETWORK. Vulnerability: NVD describes a denial-of-service condition that may let an attacker disrupt availability of the affected software path. Weakness: NVD-CWE-noinfo. Exposure: Relevant when this component validates TLS peers, update endpoints, cloud links, or other certificate-backed channels. SBOM exposure: Used by outbound TLS, update signature verification, and local encryption features. Controls: patch affected code paths, constrain exposed interfaces, and confirm watchdog/recovery behavior for availability loss. SBOM controls: Disable weak ciphers, certificate pinning, patch monitoring, crypto configuration review.
cybersecurity_findings.description_summary: NVD describes availability impact, remote exploitation context, secure-communication concern; review the CVE record for full advisory detail.
cybersecurity_findings.description_excerpt: A bug exists in the way mod_ssl handled client renegotiations. A remote attacker could send a carefully crafted request that would cause mod_ssl to enter a l...

rate_limit_policy.note: Default delay is set for NVD public API use without an API key.
